description: When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, what limitation is mentioned for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

Compatibility: Safari (iOS) requires minimum supported version 10 (Version 12 is not supported).

description: Symptom: DHCP-Server configuration status becomes stuck in pending state after configuration changes are pushed from Aruba Central, preventing proper DHCP service operation. Scenario: This issue occurs when administrators make configuration changes using Aruba Central, either through Central template groups or the UI group multi-edit option. The DHCP Server configuration status may become stuck in a pending state. This issue can occur even when the configuration changes are completely unrelated to DHCP services. The pending status persists indefinitely, requiring manual intervention.

description: Symptom: Mirror configuration with CPU as destination triggers an ops-switchd crash and system reboot. Scenario: The issue is occurs when a mirror session is configured with CPU as the destination. The crash is triggered when there is an error condition of creating the CPU mirror destination inthe hardware. Workaround: Use interface as the mirror destination.
symptom: Mirror configuration with CPU as destination triggers an ops-switchd crash and system reboot
scenario: The issue is occurs when a mirror session is configured with CPU as the destination. The crash is triggered when there is an error condition of creating the CPU mirror destination inthe hardware.
workaround: Use interface as the mirror destination.

description: Symptom: Switches may intermittently lose connectivity with Aruba Central, causing monitoring and management sessions to close unexpectedly. Scenario: This issue may occur during normal operation when Central attempts to collect monitoring data while other processes query routing information. Under certain conditions, database timeouts or session handling delays may lead to repeated connection failures.
symptom: Switches may intermittently lose connectivity with Aruba Central, causing monitoring and management sessions to close unexpectedly.

description: Symptom: Clients are unable to onboard on to a LAG interface. Scenario: This issue may occur when all interfaces are enabled on a LAG interface and the LLDP nbr is not learned on all interfaces. This disables the interfaces where LLDP nbr is learned. Workaround: Shut then no shut the port or shut all interfaces on the port and then no shut.

workaround: Shut then no shut the port or shut all interfaces on the port and then no shut.

description: Symptom: The sflow source ip configuration gets corrupted in the cache and the following debug log can be seen: 2025-11-04:11:28:04.603048 | sflowd | LOG_ERR | CDTR | 1 | SFLOW | SFLOW_CONFIG | Failed to set source IPv4. Scenario: This issue can occur when source ip is configured for sFlow and collectors are added and sFlow is enabled globally. Workaround: Unconfigure and re-configure the sFlow source ip configuration.
symptom: The sflow source ip configuration gets corrupted in the cache and the following debug log can be seen: 2025-11-04:11:28:04.603048 | sflowd | LOG_ERR | CDTR | 1 | SFLOW | SFLOW_CONFIG | Failed to set source IPv4

workaround: Unconfigure and re-configure the sFlow source ip configuration.

scenario: This issue can occur when source ip is configured for sFlow and collectors are added and sFlow is enabled globally.
workaround: Unconfigure and re-configure the sFlow source ip configuration.

sFlow (Bug ID 400963): The documented workaround is: Unconfigure and re-configure the sFlow source ip configuration.

description: Symptom: SNMPv3 user password changes do not take effect, after deleting and re-creating the user with a new password, the device still accepts the old password and rejects the new one. Scenario: This issue may occur when deleting an existing SNMPv3 user and re-adding the same username with a different 'auth-pass'; authentication succeeds only with the original password. Workaround: Create a new SNMPv3 user with the desired password and do not reuse the original username.

description: Symptom: CPU-transmitted packets are not subjected to actions configured in policies applied to route-only ports on egress. This includes traffic originating directly from the switch, such as pings, as well as traffic forwarded to the CPU as part of the initial learning of a traffic flow. Scenario: This issue occurs when a policy is applied to a route-only port on egress and CPU-transmitted packets egress the port. Workaround: If the policy has any drop actions, use an egress ACL on the port to block the traffic instead.
symptom: CPU-transmitted packets are not subjected to actions configured in policies applied to route-only ports on egress. This includes traffic originating directly from the switch, such as pings, as well as traffic forwarded to the CPU as part of the initial learning of a traffic flow.

workaround: If the policy has any drop actions, use an egress ACL on the port to block the traffic instead.

section: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
task: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
procedure: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, DL.10.xx.yyyy).
Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config.
Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.
description: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, DL.10.xx.yyyy). Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, DL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, what is the manual configuration restore procedure for software downgrade?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, DL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

procedure: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.

Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.

When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, how do you perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
